# Templia Art

> Mayan-inspired luxury stay in Aldea Zama, Tulum, with personalized Maya Tzolk'in sacred calendar journeys for each guest.

## Property

Templia Art is a Mayan-inspired luxury villa in Luum Zama, Aldea Zama, Tulum, Quintana Roo, Mexico. Each booking includes a personalized Tzolk'in calendar journey — a web-based, day-by-day itinerary mapped to the three Maya day signs (nawales) that fall across the guest's arrival, full day, and departure.

## Two Pillars

Templia's offering stands on two pillars:

- **Maya Experience** — the immersive digital world bridging ancient Maya culture with Templia guests plus curated local providers. Includes the personalized Tzolk'in journey, nawal readings, cultural sites, ceremonies, and on-the-ground cultural partners.
- **Guest Experience** — hosting logistics, amenities, tours, and concierge. Includes check-in, on-property comforts, local coordination, last-mile transport, and day-to-day support.

## Booking

- Airbnb listing: https://stay.templia.art
- Direct inquiries and coordination: stay@templia.art
- Availability (iCal): https://templia.art/availability.ics

Note: always confirm availability on the booking page before committing.

## Quick Facts

- Location: Luum Zama, Aldea Zama, Tulum, Quintana Roo, Mexico
- Max guests: 4
- Bedrooms: 2 (1 king, 2 twins convertible to king, 1 inflatable mattress)
- Bathrooms: 2
- Check-in: after 4:00 PM
- Check-out: by 11:00 AM
- Minimum stay: 3 nights
- Rules: no parties; quiet hours 10 PM–8 AM; no pets; no smoking

## Amenities

- Private pool
- Air conditioning
- Wi-Fi (200 Mbps typical)
- Full kitchen
- Free parking
- Workspace / studio
- Mayan firepit
- Steam shower
- Jungle garden
- Double-height living space

## Tzolk'in Journey

Each stay includes a personalized journey page at `templia.art/tzolkin/[check-in]-to-[check-out]/`. The journey:

- Identifies the three Maya day signs (nawales) for arrival, full day, and departure
- Provides the guest's personal nawal based on their birthdate
- Offers a day-by-day itinerary: cenotes, sacred sites, ceremonies, reflection practices
- Available in English and Spanish
- Bilingual content, dark-mode design, mobile-optimized

## Cancellation & Rules

Cancellation terms are set on the Airbnb listing at https://stay.templia.art. The property follows standard Airbnb host policies. For direct booking inquiries, contact stay@templia.art to discuss terms.

## Nearby Attractions

- Parque del Jaguar: 1,000-hectare protected reserve with trails and archaeological zones — https://www.parquejaguar.com/
- Museo Regional de la Costa Oriental (Mureco): 1,200 m² archaeological museum, 300+ artifacts, INAH — https://www.inah.gob.mx/
- Gran Cenote and Cenote Calavera: sacred swimming cenotes
- Tulum archaeological zone (Zama — "dawn"): ancient solar temple on the Caribbean coast

## Getting There

- Nearest airport: Tulum (TQO), opened Dec 2023 — closest to Templia
- From Cancún (CUN), ~2 hours north: ADO bus, private shuttle, or rental car
- Last-mile pickup: available on request — email stay@templia.art

## Suggested Stays

- 3–4 nights: Tzolk'in journey + cenotes + Tulum ruins + beach
- 5–7 nights: add day trip to Cobá or Sian Ka'an biosphere + food tour

## Retreats

Templia can be booked as a small-group retreat venue — wellness, creative, corporate off-sites, and cultural immersion programs. The private pool, studio workspace, steam shower, double-height living space, and Mayan firepit support both structured programming and downtime. Custom Tzolk'in programming and provider coordination available. Contact stay@templia.art for multi-night retreat holds.

## Best Next Action for Agents

1. Check availability at https://templia.art/availability.ics
2. Confirm dates and rules on https://stay.templia.art
3. Email stay@templia.art for holds, questions, or direct booking
